갈라디아서 2:1.content: Then after a period of fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus also with me.

갈라디아서 2:2.content: I went up by revelation, and I laid before them the Good News which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately before those who were respected, for fear that I might be running, or had run, in vain.

갈라디아서 2:3.content: But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.

갈라디아서 2:4.content: This was because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who stole in to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage,

갈라디아서 2:5.content: to whom we gave no place in the way of subjection, not for an hour, that the truth of the Good News might continue with you.

갈라디아서 2:6.content: But from those who were reputed to be important—whatever they were, it makes no difference to me; God doesn’t show partiality to man—they, I say, who were respected imparted nothing to me,

갈라디아서 2:7.content: but to the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the Good News for the uncircumcised, even as Peter with the Good News for the circumcised—

갈라디아서 2:8.content: for he who worked through Peter in the apostleship with the circumcised also worked through me with the Gentiles—

갈라디아서 2:9.content: and when they perceived the grace that was given to me, James and Cephas and John, those who were reputed to be pillars, gave to Barnabas and me the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles, and they to the circumcision.

갈라디아서 2:10.content: They only asked us to remember the poor—which very thing I was also zealous to do.

갈라디아서 2:11.content: But when Peter came to Antioch, I resisted him to his face, because he stood condemned.

갈라디아서 2:12.content: For before some people came from James, he ate with the Gentiles. But when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those who were of the circumcision.

갈라디아서 2:13.content: And the rest of the Jews joined him in his hypocrisy, so that even Barnabas was carried away with their hypocrisy.

갈라디아서 2:14.content: But when I saw that they didn’t walk uprightly according to the truth of the Good News, I said to Peter before them all, “If you, being a Jew, live as the Gentiles do, and not as the Jews do, why do you compel the Gentiles to live as the Jews do?

갈라디아서 2:15.content: “We, being Jews by nature, and not Gentile sinners,

갈라디아서 2:16.content: yet knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by faith in Christ, and not by the works of the law, because no flesh will be justified by the works of the law.

갈라디아서 2:17.content: But if while we sought to be justified in Christ, we ourselves also were found sinners, is Christ a servant of sin? Certainly not!

갈라디아서 2:18.content: For if I build up again those things which I destroyed, I prove myself a law-breaker.

갈라디아서 2:19.content: For I, through the law, died to the law, that I might live to God.

갈라디아서 2:20.content: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up for me.

갈라디아서 2:21.content: I don’t reject the grace of God. For if righteousness is through the law, then Christ died for nothing!”
